stem: Which sentence uses the present perfect tense correctly?
options: She has finished her homework already.
She finish her homework already.
She finishes her homework already.
She finished her homework already.

stem: Choose the correct article: He is ___ honest person to work with.
options: a
an
the
no article needed

stem: Select the sentence with proper subject-verb agreement.
options: Neither of the answers are correct.
One of the students were late.
Each of the cookies is baked fresh.
The team have won the match.

stem: Which option is the correct past tense form of 'to fly'?
options: flied

flown

stem: Pick the sentence that uses a conditional (Type 2) structure.
options: If it rains, we will cancel the trip.
If I knew the answer, I would tell you.
If you finished early, call me.
If she studies, she passes.

stem: Which sentence correctly uses the word 'whose'?
options: Whose jacket is this on the chair?
Who's jacket is this on the chair?
Who is jacket is this on the chair?
Whom jacket is this on the chair?

stem: Choose the correctly punctuated sentence.
options: I came, I saw I conquered.
I came; I saw, I conquered.
I came, I saw, I conquered.
I came I saw, I conquered.

stem: Select the sentence that uses the passive voice.
options: The chef cooked the meal quickly.
The meal was cooked quickly by the chef.
The chef cooks quickly every night.
The meal cooks quickly for the chef.

stem: Which word best completes the sentence? She is responsible ___ organizing the workshop.
options: of
for
with
about

stem: Choose the correct reported speech transformation: "I am learning Python," she said.
options: She said that she was learning Python.
She said that she is learning Python.
She said that she has learned Python.
She said that she learn Python.

stem: Pick the correctly capitalized sentence.
options: last summer we visited Seoul and Busan.

stem: Which sentence correctly uses a gerund?
options: To read books are my favorite hobby.
Reading books is my favorite hobby.
Read books is my favorite hobby.

stem: Select the best transition word to connect the ideas: "She was tired; ___, she kept working."
options: however
because
meanwhile
otherwise

stem: Which sentence uses the future continuous tense?
options: I will see you at the party.
I will be waiting for you at the station.

stem: Choose the sentence that correctly uses a phrasal verb.

stem: Which option correctly completes the sentence? The experiment ___ carefully documented.
options: was

stem: Pick the correct comparative structure.
options: This task is more easy than the last one.

stem: Choose the best closing sentence for a formal email.
options: Talk soon!

ample

stem: Select the sentence that correctly uses a relative clause.
